_comment: Config for risky_patterns.py. Each entry: name, regex (Python re syntax), why. Census patterns, not lint rules: hits are review pointers, expect false positives. SECURITY-SCANNER NOTE: the regex values here are detection *patterns*, not executable code — a heuristic scanner will match its own denylist and flag strings like exec(/eval(/os.system( on the lines below. That is expected and benign (this file is data, never executed); allowlist this path (skills/full-review/scripts/risky_patterns.json) in such scans.

patterns.name: eval-call
patterns.why: arbitrary code execution if input reaches it

patterns.name: exec-call
patterns.why: arbitrary code execution if input reaches it

patterns.name: shell-true
patterns.why: shell injection if input reaches the command

patterns.name: os-system
patterns.why: shell injection; prefer subprocess with list args

patterns.name: inner-html
patterns.why: XSS if input reaches it; prefer textContent

patterns.name: weak-hash
patterns.why: weak for security purposes (fine for checksums)

patterns.name: rm-rf
patterns.why: recursive force delete; verify the target is what you think

patterns.name: security-todo
patterns.why: known security gap left as a comment
